content:
# Burqan Formation

**Summary**: A sedimentary formation in the Midyan Basin, NW Saudi Arabia.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] studied the Burqan Formation to understand its depositional environment and reservoir quality.

- [[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]] characterized the Burqan Formation as a distinct unit in the Raghama sequence.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] submarine fan system

- [[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]] characterized the Burqan Formation as a distinct unit in the Raghama sequence.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] Sandstone units are friable in nature.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Nutaysh member of the Burqan Formation shows a transition from sand-dominated lower units to shale-dominated upper units. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 13]])

- Composed of fine to medium-grained sandstone beds separated by bioturbated siltstone and claystone. ([[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022, p. 43]])

## Conflicting Interpretations Across Sources

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] mechanical compaction, carbonate cementation, pyrite/hematite cementation
